title: Messy triage note with mixed ACS features
patient: Triage note: 55M here for chest pressure, started maybe ~3h ago while walking up stairs, says also hurts a bit with deep breath. PMH: HTN, smoker. Looks uncomfortable but talking full sentences. EKG from front desk says "nonspecific lateral T flattening" no big STE. Lab: trop-I 0.09, ref <0.04. Random note: skipped breakfast, worried about missing work tomorrow.

title: Out-of-order ED note with pending troponin
patient: ED quick note: chest pressure w exertion today, got better sitting down. 52 yo male. PMH HTN. Dad MI at 50. ECG "NSR no acute ST-T" per machine read. Labs pending - troponin not back yet, nurse called lab. Pt asking if he can leave before pickup window closes.

title: STEMI shorthand note from resus bay
patient: 58M brought in clutching chest, sweaty, pale. Pain x40 min, crushing substernal. ECG done immediately: STE II/III/aVF with reciprocal STD I,aVL. PMH: DM + smoker. Trop drawn but not needed to trigger pathway. Team already paging cards.

title: Noisy triage prose with PE clues
patient: 25F c/o sharp R chest pain worse breathing in, started this afternoon. just got off long car trip (8h). denies cardiac hx. pulse 102 at triage. EKG sinus tachy, no ST/T ischemic changes. trop 0.01 (<0.04). Also says she forgot lunch and has finals this week.

title: Known CAD note with old Q waves called out
patient: 67M known CAD/CABG, here w exertional substernal pressure radiating L arm, says "same as prior angina". Risks: HTN + DM. ECG: old inferior Q waves, no NEW ST changes per attending. trop-I 0.03 (ULN 0.04). Wife asking if this can be handled outpatient.

title: Chaotic sign-out style note with instability
patient: Sign-out: 60M crushing CP + diaphoresis, now confused, BP 76/40 repeat 78/42. prior MI, DM, HTN. ECG not clean but diffuse ischemic-looking ST changes. trop back high 0.30 (<0.04). Charge RN already moving to higher acuity bay.
